message:
feat: injectable time sources for deterministic testing across H2, TCP, shutdown, deadline monitor, and connection manager

Replace ambient Instant::now() and wall_now() calls with injectable fn() -> Time/Instant
time sources across five major subsystems. This enables fully deterministic unit tests
that previously relied on thread::sleep or Instant arithmetic hacks to simulate time
progression.

Key changes per subsystem:

- H2 Connection: Add `client_with_time_getter` / `server_with_time_getter` constructors.
  Replace all Instant::now() in continuation timeout, RST_STREAM rate limiting, and
  push-promise tracking with `(self.time_getter)()`. Tests now use a shared atomic
  test clock with `lock_test_clock()` serialization to avoid cross-test interference.

- TCP Stream: Extract `connect_timeout_with_time_getter` and new `future_with_timeout`
  helper that drives TimeoutFuture via poll_fn with a custom clock. Add
  `timeout_now_with_fallback` so the no-runtime path can also be tested deterministically.
  New tests: `future_with_timeout_honors_custom_clock`,
  `future_with_timeout_completes_before_deadline`.

- Deadline Monitor: Add `with_time_getter` constructor. Scan interval checks now use
  injected clock. Test helpers: `test_now()`, `test_now_minus()`, `lock_test_clock()`.
  Existing tests (`warns_on_approaching_deadline`, `warns_on_no_progress`) migrated to
  use injected clock.

- Shutdown Signal: Convert `current_time()` from static method to instance method
  backed by stored `time_getter`. Add `has_drain_deadline` / `has_drain_start` AtomicBool
  flags to correctly distinguish "deadline is Time(0)" from "no deadline set". New test:
  `with_time_getter_controls_deadline_and_duration`.

- Connection Manager: Add `sleep_until_with_time_getter` async helper. Drain loop now
  uses `self.shutdown_signal.current_time()` instead of static `ShutdownSignal::current_time()`.
  New test: `drain_with_stats_timeout_uses_injected_shutdown_clock`.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
